items.title: COLORS-IN-DESIGN.TXT
items.body:
How to choose the right colors for your website

Colors aren't just decoration. They're a tool that influences how people perceive your site.

Basic rule: less is more. Pick 2-3 main colors and stick to them. One dominant, one for accents, one neutral.

Common mistakes:
- Too many colors at once
- Poor contrast between text and background
- Ignoring color psychology

Blue builds trust. Green means growth. Red is energy. Black is elegance.

But context matters most. Colors must fit your brand, not trends.

Don't forget accessibility. 4.5:1 contrast for text, 3:1 for UI elements. Your grandma should read it as easily as you.

— Bits&Bytes

items.title: TYPOGRAPHY-BASICS.TXT
items.body:
Typography: the foundation of good web design

People read text, not graphics. That's why typography is the most important part of design.

Basic rules:

1. Two fonts are enough
One for headings, one for text. More is chaos.

2. Right size
Text minimum 16px. Headings significantly larger. Mobile users will thank you.

3. Line height
Text needs to breathe. 1.5-1.8 for body text is standard.

4. Line length
50-75 characters per line. More tires, less bores.

5. Contrast
Black on white works. Gray on gray doesn't.

The best typography is the one nobody notices. Because people just read, without obstacles.

Choose a font that fits your brand. Modern? Sans-serif. Traditional? Serif. Technical? Monospace.

And most importantly: test on real devices.

— Bits&Bytes
